devanagari: ऋषिरुवाच । इति तासां स्वशक्तीनां सतीनामसमेत्य सः । प्रसुप्तलोकतन्त्राणां निशाम्य गतिमीश्वरः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣiruvācaiti tāsāṃ svaśaktīnāṃ satīnāmasametya saḥprasuptalokatantrāṇāṃ niśāmya gatimīśvaraḥ

devanagari: कालसञ्ज्ञां तदा देवीं बिभ्रत् शक्तिमुरुक्रमः । त्रयोविंशति तत्त्वानां गणं युगपदाविशत् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: kālasañjñāṃ tadā devīṃ bibhrat śaktimurukramaḥtrayoviṃśati tattvānāṃ gaṇaṃ yugapadāviśat

devanagari: सोऽनुप्रविष्टो भगवान् चेष्टारूपेण तं गणम् । भिन्नं संयोजयामास सुप्तं कर्म प्रबोधयन् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: so'nupraviṣṭo bhagavān ceṣṭārūpeṇa taṃ gaṇambhinnaṃ saṃyojayāmāsa suptaṃ karma prabodhayan
translation: The Lord who entered the group (of 23 principles) made the separate principles to work together by his power of action (kriyā-śakti) rousing up their dormant working capacity.[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Karma—The actions or fate in store (??ṛṣṭa [adṛṣṭa?]) of jīvas.

devanagari: प्रबुद्धकर्म दैवेन त्रयोविंशतिको गणः । प्रेरितोऽजनयत्स्वाभिः मात्राभिः अधिपूरुषम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: prabuddhakarma daivena trayoviṃśatiko gaṇaḥprerito'janayatsvābhiḥ mātrābhiḥ adhipūruṣam
translation: The group of twenty-three principles, the potency of which to work is thus roused by the Lord, and which were thus impelled by him, created the Virāṭ Puruṣa by contributing their individual parts.

devanagari: परेण विशता स्वस्मिन् मात्रया विश्वसृग्गणः । चुक्षोभान्योन्यमासाद्य यस्मिन् लोकाश्चराचराः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: pareṇa viśatā svasmin mātrayā viśvasṛggaṇaḥcukṣobhānyonyamāsādya yasmin lokāścarācarāḥ
translation: The group of the creative principles of the universe, due to the entry of only a part (aṃśa) of the Supreme Lord in them, became assimilated with each other and were in commotion leading to the creation (of the Virāṭ Puruṣa) in which are the movables and immovables.

devanagari: हिरण्मयः स पुरुषः सहस्रपरिवत्सरान् । आण्डकोश उवासाप्सु सर्वसत्त्वोपबृंहितः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: hiraṇmayaḥ sa puruṣaḥ sahasraparivatsarānāṇḍakośa uvāsāpsu sarvasattvopabṛṃhitaḥ
translation: That Virāṭ Puruṣa of gold (of the complexion of gold) containing all the beings (in invisible forms) stayed in the egg (called Brahmāṇḍa) on cosmic waters for one thousand years[3].

devanagari: स वै विश्वसृजां गर्भो देवकर्मात्मशक्तिमान् । विबभाजात्मनात्मानं एकधा दशधा त्रिधा ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai viśvasṛjāṃ garbho devakarmātmaśaktimānvibabhājātmanātmānaṃ ekadhā daśadhā tridhā
translation: Verily he (the Vitāṭ Puruṣa), the offspring (the product) of the principles of the universe, the possessor of the divine potency of knowledge, action and spirit,[4] divided[5] himself as one (in the form of the spirit residing in the heart), as ten (in the from of the vital airs—Prāṇas—viz. Prāṇa, apāna, vyāna, udāna, samāna, nāga, kūrma, kṛkala, devadatta and dhanañjaya), and as three (viz. forms related to adhibhūta, adhidaiva and adhyātma).
purport:
Footnote [4]:
deva-karmātma-śaktimān—According to VṚ. these powers are: Jīva śakti or avidyā, the karma of jīvas and the spirit or intelligent power (cit śakti) useful in the creation of the body and other conditions of jīva. Padaratnāvalī reads ‘daiva-’ for ‘deva’ and interpreting garbha as the Inner controller (antaryāmin) of the principles e.g. mahat and others, enumerates the potencies as (1) Power superior to gods like Brahmā, (2) the fate (adṛṣṭa) of jīvas and (3) Prakṛti the medium of enjoying pleasure, pain etc.

Footnote [5]:
This division is clarified in verse 9 below.

devanagari: एष ह्यशेषसत्त्वानां आत्मांशः परमात्मनः । आद्योऽवतारो यत्रासौ भूतग्रामो विभाव्यते ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa hyaśeṣasattvānāṃ ātmāṃśaḥ paramātmanaḥādyo'vatāro yatrāsau bhūtagrāmo vibhāvyate
translation: This[6] (Virāṭ puruṣā) is the individual soul within all beings. He himself is a part of the Supreme Soul (paramātman). He is the first incarnation (avatāra) in whom is supported the multitude of all beings.[7]
purport:
Footnote [6]:
?ṣaḥ [eṣaḥ?]—Bhāgavata Candrikā thinks that this refers to god Brahmā who is the support of all individual beings, and who is an incarnation (avatāra) of the ‘possession’ (āveśa) type. Padaratnāvalī states that word eṣa refers to Hari in the Puruṣa incarnation. On the support of the Puruṣa depends the creation and preservation of all beings.

Footnote [7]:
It is in the body of the Virāṭ (Puruṣa) that the whole of the universe consisting of mobiles and immobiles is seen—Bālaprabodhini

devanagari: साध्यात्मः साधिदैवश्च साधिभूत इति त्रिधा । विराट्प्राणो दशविध एकधा हृदयेन च ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: sādhyātmaḥ sādhidaivaśca sādhibhūta iti tridhāvirāṭprāṇo daśavidha ekadhā hṛdayena ca
translation: The Virāṭ Puruṣa (God Brahma) has three forms. viz. those related with adhyātma, adhidaiva and adhibhūta[8], ten forms viz. the ten vital airs (prāṇas) and one form, viz. that connected with the heart.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
According to Bhāgavata Candrikā sādhyātma = with jīva (indweller of the body). Sādhidaiva = with the senses in the samaṣṭi stage as in the Sun etc. Sādhibhūta = with sounds etc. which are the characteristics of elements (bhūtas).

devanagari: स्मरन्विश्वसृजामीशो विज्ञापितं अधोक्षजः । विराजं अतपत्स्वेन तेजसैषां विवृत्तये ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: smaranviśvasṛjāmīśo vijñāpitaṃ adhokṣajaḥvirājaṃ atapatsvena tejasaiṣāṃ vivṛttaye
translation: [9] Lord Viṣṇu (adhokṣaja), remembering the prayer (vide supra 3.5.48) of (the presiding deities of) the Principles of creating the universe, planned (the person of) the Virāṭ (or Hiraṇyagarbha) by his power of knowledge (or spiritual light), for enabling them to perform their functions.
purport:
Footnote [9]:
Lord Viṣṇu entered the person of Virāṭ (God Brahmā) as an antaryāmin (controller from within). He enlarged the body of Virāj (god Brahmā) to provide accommodation to the deities (such as Fire etc.)—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: अथ तस्याभितप्तस्य कति चायतनानि ह । निरभिद्यन्त देवानां तानि मे गदतः श्रृणु ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: atha tasyābhitaptasya kati cāyatanāni hanirabhidyanta devānāṃ tāni me gadataḥ śrṛṇu
translation: Now, hear from me, how many places of residence of gods (Presiding deities of sense-organs) were created in the body of Virāṭ by his penance, viz. the power of his knowledge.

devanagari: तस्याग्निरास्यं निर्भिन्नं लोकपालोऽविशत्पदम् । वाचा स्वांशेन वक्तव्यं ययासौ प्रतिपद्यते ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāgnirāsyaṃ nirbhinnaṃ lokapālo'viśatpadamvācā svāṃśena vaktavyaṃ yayāsau pratipadyate
translation: [10] (When) his mouth was differentiated, (formed) Fire, the protector of the world, entered it along with his power called speech. Hence jīvas (people) get the power of speech.

devanagari: निर्भिन्नं तालु वरुणो लोकपालोऽविशद्धरेः । जिह्वयांशेन च रसं ययासौ प्रतिपद्यते ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: nirbhinnaṃ tālu varuṇo lokapālo'viśaddhareḥjihvayāṃśena ca rasaṃ yayāsau pratipadyate
translation: Palate was evolved in Hari (the Virāṭ Puruṣa). The protector of the world, called Varuṇa, entered it along with part (aṃśa) called tongue (the conative sense-organ of taste) whereby the jīva attains the power of taste.

devanagari: निर्भिन्ने अश्विनौ नासे विष्णोः आविशतां पदम् । घ्राणेनांशेन गन्धस्य प्रतिपत्तिर्यतो भवेत् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: nirbhinne aśvinau nāse viṣṇoḥ āviśatāṃ padamghrāṇenāṃśena gandhasya pratipattiryato bhavet
translation: (When) the nostrils of Viṣṇu (the Virāṭ Puruṣa) were formed, the two Aśvinikumāras occupied (entered) them by their power, viz. the sense of smell, whereby the jīva attained the capacity to smell.

devanagari: निर्भिन्ने अक्षिणी त्वष्टा लोकपालोऽविशद्विभोः । चक्षुषांशेन रूपाणां प्रतिपत्तिर्यतो भवेत् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: nirbhinne akṣiṇī tvaṣṭā lokapālo'viśadvibhoḥcakṣuṣāṃśena rūpāṇāṃ pratipattiryato bhavet
translation: (Then) the two eyes (of the Virāṭ Puruṣa) were formed. The Sun, a protector of the world, entered them with his power, viz., the sense of sight. Hence the jīvas get the perception of forms and colours by the eyes.

devanagari: निर्भिन्नान्यस्य चर्माणि लोकपालोऽनिलोऽविशत् । प्राणेनांशेन संस्पर्शं येनासौ प्रतिपद्यते ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: nirbhinnānyasya carmāṇi lokapālo'nilo'viśatprāṇenāṃśena saṃsparśaṃ yenāsau pratipadyate
translation: (Then the Virāṭ Puruṣa had) his skins developed. The protector of the world called Vāyu entered it along with his power of breath (Prāṇa) whereby the jīva gets the sense of touch.

devanagari: कर्णौ अस्य विनिर्भिन्नौ धिष्ण्यं स्वं विविशुर्दिशः । श्रोत्रेणांशेन शब्दस्य सिद्धिं येन प्रपद्यते ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: karṇau asya vinirbhinnau dhiṣṇyaṃ svaṃ viviśurdiśaḥśrotreṇāṃśena śabdasya siddhiṃ yena prapadyate
translation: (When) his two ears were evolved, the (presiding deities of) quarters along with their power of audition, entered them as their place, whereby he (the jīva) can hear the sound.

devanagari: त्वचमस्य विनिर्भिन्नां विविशुर्धिष्ण्यमोषधीः । अंशेन रोमभिः कण्डूं यैरसौ प्रतिपद्यते ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tvacamasya vinirbhinnāṃ viviśurdhiṣṇyamoṣadhīḥaṃśena romabhiḥ kaṇḍūṃ yairasau pratipadyate
translation: [11] (When) the skin was evolved (of the Virāṭ Puruṣa), the presiding deities of herbs (and plants) occupied it as their place, by their parts called hair, whereby the jīva can feel the itching-sensation.
purport:
Footnote [11]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā is conscious of the duplication of tvac and carman (in verses 16 and 18) and explains it as the difference of place and difference in functions, viz. touch and itch. Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa explains that the outer part of the skin is carman and tvac is related both to its inside and outside.

devanagari: मेढ्रं तस्य विनिर्भिन्नं स्वधिष्ण्यं क उपाविशत् । रेतसांशेन येनासौ आनन्दं प्रतिपद्यते ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: meḍhraṃ tasya vinirbhinnaṃ svadhiṣṇyaṃ ka upāviśatretasāṃśena yenāsau ānandaṃ pratipadyate
translation: Then his penis was evolved. God Ka (i.e. Prajāpati) along with his power—semen—entered it as his residence. (It is) by this (organ) that the jīva (or the world) gets (sexual) pleasure.

devanagari: गुदं पुंसो विनिर्भिन्नं मित्रो लोकेश आविशत् । पायुनांशेन येनासौ विसर्गं प्रतिपद्यते ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: gudaṃ puṃso vinirbhinnaṃ mitro lokeśa āviśatpāyunāṃśena yenāsau visargaṃ pratipadyate
translation: Then the anus of the Virāṭ Puruṣa was evolved. Mitra, the controller (protector) of the world, along with his part (known as) Pāyu (anus), entered it. (It is) by this that the jīva has the organ of excretion.

devanagari: हस्तावस्य विनिर्भिन्नौ इन्द्रः स्वर्पतिराविशत् । वार्तयांशेन पुरुषो यया वृत्तिं प्रपद्यते ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: hastāvasya vinirbhinnau indraḥ svarpatirāviśatvārtayāṃśena puruṣo yayā vṛttiṃ prapadyate
translation: His hands were (then) developed. Indra, the lord of the Heaven (Svarga), along with his power of making sales and purchases, entered them, whereby a man can earn his livelihood.

devanagari: पादौ अस्य विनिर्भिन्नौ लोकेशो विष्णुराविशत् । गत्या स्वांशेन पुरुषो यया प्राप्यं प्रपद्यते ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: pādau asya vinirbhinnau lokeśo viṣṇurāviśatgatyā svāṃśena puruṣo yayā prāpyaṃ prapadyate
translation: Then his feet were evolved. Viṣṇu, the Lord of the world, entered them along with his power of locomotion, by which man can reach his desired place.

devanagari: बुद्धिं चास्य विनिर्भिन्नां वागीशो धिष्ण्यमाविशत् । बोधेनांशेन बोद्धव्य प्रतिपत्तिर्यतो भवेत् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: buddhiṃ cāsya vinirbhinnāṃ vāgīśo dhiṣṇyamāviśatbodhenāṃśena boddhavya pratipattiryato bhavet
translation: And intellect was evolved in him. God Brahmā, with his power of accurate perception, entered it as his place. (It is) by this faculty that the object of knowledge is comprehended.

devanagari: हृदयं चास्य निर्भिन्नं चन्द्रमा धिष्ण्यमाविशत् । मनसांशेन येनासौ विक्रियां प्रतिपद्यते ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: hṛdayaṃ cāsya nirbhinnaṃ candramā dhiṣṇyamāviśatmanasāṃśena yenāsau vikriyāṃ pratipadyate
translation: And his heart was evolved. The moon, along with his faculty called mind, entered it as his abode. Hence the jīva (or the world) understands changes (in the state of mind, thoughts etc.).

devanagari: आत्मानं चास्य निर्भिन्नं अभिमानोऽविशत्पदम् । कर्मणांशेन येनासौ कर्तव्यं प्रतिपद्यते ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmānaṃ cāsya nirbhinnaṃ abhimāno'viśatpadamkarmaṇāṃśena yenāsau kartavyaṃ pratipadyate
translation: [12] God Rudra (abhimāna) along with his capacity of action (or principle of activity) entered as his place, the evolved egohood (ātman) of Virāṭ Puruṣa. It is by this (ego-hood) that jīva executes his work (as his own).
purport:
Footnote [12]:
Padaratnāvalī follows a different text as shown by his interpretation: Then the special nāḍī called ātmanāḍī was evolved. Bṛhaspati with his faculty of intelligence entered it. Hereby god Brahmā arrives at correct conclusion.

devanagari: सत्त्वं चास्य विनिर्भिन्नं महान् धिष्ण्यमुपाविशत् । चित्तेनांशेन येनासौ विज्ञानं प्रतिपद्यते ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: sattvaṃ cāsya vinirbhinnaṃ mahān dhiṣṇyamupāviśatcittenāṃśena yenāsau vijñānaṃ pratipadyate
translation: And his sattva[13] (mind and intellect was evolved). Mahat (god Brahmā)occupied this abode along with citta whereby he (the jīva) arrives at comprehension (understanding).[14]
purport:
Footnote [13]:
Sattva—The nāḍī called sattva—Padaratnāvalī
The inner organ called Buddhi and citta—Bhāgavata Candrikā

Footnote [14]:
Vijñānam [Vijñāna]—Correct understanding of what should be done—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: शीर्ष्णोऽस्य द्यौर्धरा पद्‍भ्यां खं नाभेरुदपद्यत । गुणानां वृत्तयो येषु प्रतीयन्ते सुरादयः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: śīrṣṇo'sya dyaurdharā pad‍bhyāṃ khaṃ nābherudapadyataguṇānāṃ vṛttayo yeṣu pratīyante surādayaḥ
translation: From his (Virāṭ Puruṣa’s) head was produced the svar-loka. The earth came forth from his feet, and the sky from his navel. The products (effects) of guṇas (like sattva) such as gods and others are seen in these (regions).[15]
purport:
Footnote [15]:
Gods, men etc. possessing the guṇas like sattva, rajas and tamas are seen (i.e. created) in these regions—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) Beings in whom the states of happiness, activity etc. which are the characteristics of guṇas (like sattva etc.) are found in these regions.—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: आत्यन्तिकेन सत्त्वेन दिवं देवाः प्रपेदिरे । धरां रजःस्वभावेन पणयो ये च ताननु ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: ātyantikena sattvena divaṃ devāḥ prapediredharāṃ rajaḥsvabhāvena paṇayo ye ca tānanu
translation: Gods attained the Heaven by their excellence of sattva attribute. Due to their nature predominated by sattva, Paṇis (i.e. men who deal with gods by performance of sacrifices) and their belongings (like cows etc.) remained on the earth.

devanagari: तार्तीयेन स्वभावेन भगवन् नाभिमाश्रिताः । उभयोरन्तरं व्योम ये रुद्रपार्षदां गणाः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tārtīyena svabhāvena bhagavan nābhimāśritāḥubhayorantaraṃ vyoma ye rudrapārṣadāṃ gaṇāḥ
translation: Due to their nature characterised by the third (guṇa viz. tamas), the mass of the followers of Rudṛa resorted to the Lord’s navel, viz. the space between the two regions mentioned above.

devanagari: मुखतोऽवर्तत ब्रह्म पुरुषस्य कुरूद्वह । यस्तून्मुखत्वाद् वर्णानां मुख्योऽभूद् ब्राह्मणो गुरुः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: mukhato'vartata brahma puruṣasya kurūdvahayastūnmukhatvād varṇānāṃ mukhyo'bhūd brāhmaṇo guruḥ
translation: [16] Oh leader of Kurus! From the mouth of the Puruṣa came forth Brahman (the Veda) and the Brāhmaṇa class like syllables coming out from the mouth (head). Hence the Brāhmaṇa Varṇa became the foremost among the Varṇas.
purport:
Footnote [16]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā explains that as the Vedas (Brahma) which is the subsistence of the Brāhmaṇa class, came forth from the mouth (or head), the.Brāhmaṇa Varṇa became the chief (head) class (Varṇa) of all.

devanagari: बाहुभ्योऽवर्तत क्षत्रं क्षत्रियस्तदनुव्रतः । यो जातस्त्रायते वर्णान् पौरुषः कण्टकक्षतात् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: bāhubhyo'vartata kṣatraṃ kṣatriyastadanuvrataḥyo jātastrāyate varṇān pauruṣaḥ kaṇṭakakṣatāt
translation: From his arms emanated the power of protection and the Kṣatriya class who follows that vow, viz. the duty of protecting the world. This class born from Puruṣa (Lord Viṣṇu) protects the classes of people from wounds (i.e. injuries or troubles) caused by thorns (in the form of miscreants).

devanagari: विशोऽवर्तन्त तस्योर्वोः लोकवृत्तिकरीर्विभोः । वैश्यस्तदुद्‍भवो वार्तां नृणां यः समवर्तयत् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: viśo'vartanta tasyorvoḥ lokavṛttikarīrvibhoḥvaiśyastadud‍bhavo vārtāṃ nṛṇāṃ yaḥ samavartayat
translation: From the thighs of that All-pervading Lord were born the vocations like agriculture which maintain the livelihood of the public. The Vaiśya class, born from the same part of the body, carries out trades and agriculture for the maintenance of people.

devanagari: पद्‍भ्यां भगवतो जज्ञे शुश्रूषा धर्मसिद्धये । तस्यां जातः पुरा शूद्रो यद्वृत्त्या तुष्यते हरिः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: pad‍bhyāṃ bhagavato jajñe śuśrūṣā dharmasiddhayetasyāṃ jātaḥ purā śūdro yadvṛttyā tuṣyate hariḥ
translation: From the feet of the Lord was born service for the achievement of religion. Formerly the Śūdra class was born for the sake of service, whereby Hari is pleased.

devanagari: एते वर्णाः स्वधर्मेण यजन्ति स्वगुरुं हरिम् । श्रद्धया आत्मविशुद्ध्यर्थं यज्जाताः सह वृत्तिभिः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: ete varṇāḥ svadharmeṇa yajanti svaguruṃ harimśraddhayā ātmaviśuddhyarthaṃ yajjātāḥ saha vṛttibhiḥ
translation: These classes (Brāhmaṇas etc.) who are born along with their means of livelihood (and duties) from Hari, worship with faith their creator and master Hari for self-purification, by following the path of their duties.

devanagari: एतत् क्षत्तर्भगवतो दैवकर्मात्मरूपिणः । कः श्रद्दध्यादुपाकर्तुं योगमायाबलोदयम् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: etat kṣattarbhagavato daivakarmātmarūpiṇaḥkaḥ śraddadhyādupākartuṃ yogamāyābalodayam
translation: Oh Vidura! Who can even dare to wish (confidently) to describe fully in details this (universe) which has been created by the yoga-māyā of the Almighty Lord who assumes the forms of kāla (time), karma (action) and svabhāva (nature).

devanagari: तथापि कीर्तयाम्यङ्ग यथामति यथाश्रुतम् । कीर्तिं हरेः स्वां सत्कर्तुं गिरमन्याभिधासतीम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: tathāpi kīrtayāmyaṅga yathāmati yathāśrutamkīrtiṃ hareḥ svāṃ satkartuṃ giramanyābhidhāsatīm
translation: It is for purifying my tongue of the impurities caused by uttering things unrelated with Hari that I, however, describe the glory of Hari to the best of my intellect, according as I have heard of it.

devanagari: एकान्तलाभं वचसो नु पुंसां । सुश्लोकमौलेर्गुणवादमाहुः । श्रुतेश्च विद्वद्भिरुपाकृतायां । कथासुधायां उपसम्प्रयोगम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: ekāntalābhaṃ vacaso nu puṃsāṃsuślokamaulerguṇavādamāhuḥśruteśca vidvadbhirupākṛtāyāṃkathāsudhāyāṃ upasamprayogam
translation: They say that the description of the excellent qualities of him who is the best amongst personages of holy fame, brings the highest benefit to the power of speech of men, and listening to the nectarlike stories (of Hari) as described by the learned is the greatest benefit of the power of hearing.

devanagari: आत्मनोऽवसितो वत्स महिमा कविनाऽदिना । संवत्सरसहस्रान्ते धिया योगविपक्वया ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmano'vasito vatsa mahimā kavinā'dināsaṃvatsarasahasrānte dhiyā yogavipakvayā
translation: Oh child! Was the greatness of Lord Hari thoroughly comprehended by the First Sage (Brahmā) with his intelligence matured by yoga after (performing penance for) one thousand years?

devanagari: अतो भगवतो माया मायिनामपि मोहिनी । यत्स्वयं चात्मवर्त्मात्मा न वेद किमुतापरे ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: ato bhagavato māyā māyināmapi mohinīyatsvayaṃ cātmavartmātmā na veda kimutāpare
translation: The Māyā of the Lord deludes even the possessor of Māyā so much that the Lord himself does not know the course of his Māyā. What of others?

devanagari: यतोऽप्राप्य न्यवर्तन्त वाचश्च मनसा सह । अहं चान्य इमे देवाः तस्मै भगवते नमः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: yato'prāpya nyavartanta vācaśca manasā sahaahaṃ cānya ime devāḥ tasmai bhagavate namaḥ
translation: Salutations to the Almighty Lord without reaching whom (without realizing whose glory) the Vedas (or the powers of speech), mind (though purified by yoga), Rudra (ego-hood) and other deities presiding over organs of senses, have returned.
